title: Russia Historical Context Room

fields.vision: The Russia Desk is a two-page analytical workbench: a contemporary security desk for regime security, military adaptation, influence operations, Ukraine, NATO, and strategic decision-making; and a history page for the longer imperial, Soviet, post-Soviet, and European-order inheritance that gives present judgment depth.
fields.mechanism: The mechanism is source-lane to judgment-lane conversion. Internal workbench files, Kindle/Routledge/source library source families, journal and transcript manifests, marker packs, visual dossiers, exact-source queues, and source lineage bridges are separated by role.
fields.built_components: Built components include four source-root lanes, nine manifest lanes, an active marker register, a visual-source binding dossier, five named subrooms, a page source plan, an exact-source review packet, a public source-use section, and priority source lineageability cards. This is enough to show a serious workbench foundation. It is not enough to claim final source check.
fields.source_utilization: Source families are assigned burdens rather than listed decoratively: strategic-culture and regime-security literature frames statecraft; Russia-Europe/NATO scholarship frames security architecture; Ukraine and military-analysis sources separate escalation, coercion, and alliance response; influence-operations sources prevent social-media reductionism; historical and archival reservoirs give the history page long-range depth; practitioner transcripts stress-test academic claims against operational vocabulary.
fields.plan: Next plan: adjudicate the 267 matched exact-source rows, beginning with the 8 P0 rows; admit or reject source library candidate passages with evidence class and limitation; finish missing public-page proof packets; record human visual calibration; then narrow or rewrite any public claim whose source chain does not support its current wording.
fields.status: Status is honest and deliberately constrained: the source lineage foundation exists and the public page can show substantial analytic architecture, but the latest Russia proof state remains FAIL_UPDATED_V6_FULL_LAYER_COVERAGE because source lineage checking and several updated v6 visual/storytelling proof files remain open.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: exact-source decision completion, human visual calibration, missing public proof files, final History Page sequencing, tighter subroom-specific source plans, and a final external-reader pass to make sure each Russia claim is precise, non-sensational, and not stronger than the source record.
fields.detailed_source_vision: The Russia Desk is a two-page analytical workbench: a contemporary security desk for regime security, military adaptation, influence operations, Ukraine, NATO, and strategic decision-making; and a history page for the longer imperial, Soviet, post-Soviet, and European-order inheritance that gives present judgment depth. Its purpose is not to produce a generic Russia overview. It is to discipline Russia analysis across current picture, power mapping, briefing synthesis, judgment control, and release governance.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The mechanism is source-lane to judgment-lane conversion. Internal workbench files, Kindle/Routledge/source library source families, journal and transcript manifests, marker packs, visual dossiers, exact-source queues, and source lineage bridges are separated by role.

source_derived_signals.text: Select a pivot at left to inspect its continuity feature, counterfactual branch, and source citations. The afterimage of the room — six pivots, one continuity — emerges from reading them in sequence.

Phase A: cell match logic uses pivot-feature heuristic (provisional). Phase B reads per-cell continuity assertions from corpus dossier with citation chips. Phase C adds illumination-kindle on cell hover.
Phase A skeleton · 2026-05-01 · register specialization in Phase B · animations + chrysography in Phase C

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: The Russia Desk is a two-page analytical workbench: a contemporary security desk for regime security, military adaptation, influence operations, Ukraine, NATO, and strategic decision-making; and a history page for the longer imperial, Soviet, post-Soviet, and European-order inheritance that gives present judgment depth.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: The mechanism is source-lane to judgment-lane conversion. Internal workbench files, Kindle/Routledge/source library source families, journal and transcript manifests, marker packs, visual dossiers, exact-source queues, and source lineage bridges are separated by role.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It links regional knowledge to analytic method by preserving source lineage, strategic frame, adversary logic, and claim limits for later Russia/security work.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

instruments.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Russia Source Lane; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.
